rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and data types.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees, avoiding implementation-specific subcomponent paths.
generic_rules: Declare helper variables with explicit types matching the expression semantics (bool, int, real) to avoid type mismatch errors.
Generate range assumptions using named constants or visible feature names when concrete bounds are provided.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs without referencing implementation-only paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only identifiers that are in scope for the component type or implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-specific paths in component type contracts.
Avoid generating clauses that depend on AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens such as ms/us/Hz/Bytes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints not directly tied to visible ports or data types
Implementation-level bindings and assignments outside of component type scopes
Validator diagnostics and repair strategies for AGREE syntax errors
